controls.title: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure — including end-user devices, network devices, IoT devices, and servers — so that only authorized assets are given access and unauthorized and unmanaged assets are found and prevented from gaining access.
evidence: Enterprise asset inventory records showing all hardware devices discovered, classified by type, owner, and location, with timestamps of last seen activity
Network scanning or NAC reports demonstrating automated discovery of connected devices and identification of unauthorized assets
Asset management platform configuration showing scheduled discovery scans and reconciliation against the authorized asset register
Process documentation and records for onboarding and offboarding assets, including decommission evidence for retired devices

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
Address Unauthorized Assets
Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
Use DHCP Logging to Update Enterprise Asset Inventory
Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool

controls.title: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.description: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all software on the network so that only authorized software is installed and can execute, and that unauthorized and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.
evidence: Software asset inventory listing all authorized applications installed across the enterprise, including version numbers, license status, and responsible owner
Application allowlisting or software whitelisting policy and associated tool reports showing enforcement against unauthorized software execution
Software usage and license management reports identifying unlicensed or end-of-life software with remediation records
Configuration management database (CMDB) records correlating software assets to hardware assets and business owners

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
Address Unauthorized Software
Utilize Automated Software Inventory Tools
Allowlist Authorized Software
Allowlist Authorized Libraries
Allowlist Authorized Scripts

controls.title: Data Protection
controls.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data in all its formats in order to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction throughout its lifecycle.
evidence: Data classification policy defining sensitivity tiers (e.g., public, internal, confidential, restricted) with examples of data types in each category
Encryption-at-rest configuration evidence for databases, file shares, and endpoint storage showing algorithm strength and key management procedures
DLP solution policy reports and alert/incident logs demonstrating enforcement of data handling rules for sensitive data categories
Data retention and disposal records including certificate of destruction for decommissioned media containing sensitive information
Data masking or tokenization implementation records for non-production environments and external-facing integrations handling sensitive data

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory
Configure Data Access Control Lists
Enforce Data Retention
Securely Dispose of Data
Encrypt Data on End-User Devices
Establish and Maintain a Data Classification Scheme
Document Data Flows
Encrypt Data on Removable Media
Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
Segment Data Processing and Storage Based on Sensitivity
Deploy a Data Loss Prevention Solution
Log Sensitive Data Access

controls.title: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets and software — including operating systems, applications, network devices, and cloud infrastructure — to reduce the attack surface and prevent exploitation of default or misconfigured settings.
evidence: Hardening standards documented for each platform type (Windows, Linux, network devices, cloud services) referencing CIS Benchmarks or equivalent baselines
Configuration compliance scan results showing percentage of systems meeting the approved hardening baseline, with remediation tracking for findings
Change management records for configuration changes to enterprise assets, including approval, testing, and rollback procedures
Automated configuration management tool (e.g., Ansible, SCCM, Chef) deployment evidence showing baseline enforcement and drift detection alerts

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process for Network Infrastructure
Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
Implement and Manage a Firewall on End-User Devices
Securely Manage Enterprise Assets and Software
Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
Configure Trusted DNS Servers on Enterprise Assets
Enforce Automatic Device Lockout on Portable End-User Devices
Enforce Remote Wipe Capability on Portable End-User Devices
Separate Enterprise Workspaces on Mobile End-User Devices

controls.title: Account Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorization to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to mitigate the risk of unauthorized access through compromised, shared, or inappropriately privileged credentials.
evidence: User account provisioning and deprovisioning records including access request approvals, role assignments, and timely termination evidence for departed users
Periodic access review records (at minimum quarterly for privileged accounts) showing management sign-off and remediation of inappropriate access
Service account inventory with documented business justification, least-privilege configuration, and ownership assignment for each account
Privileged access management (PAM) solution reports showing session recording, just-in-time access grants, and credential vaulting for administrative accounts

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
Use Unique Passwords
Disable Dormant Accounts
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Accounts
Centralize Account Management

controls.title: Access Control Management
controls.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts for enterprise assets and software, enforcing the principle of least privilege and requiring strong authentication for all access.
evidence: MFA enrollment reports showing coverage across user populations, with attestation that MFA is enforced for remote access, cloud platforms, and privileged actions
Role-based access control (RBAC) model documentation and implementation evidence showing least-privilege role assignments mapped to job functions
Access control policy and enforcement records for sensitive systems, including conditional access policies restricting access by device compliance, location, and risk score
SSO platform configuration showing centralized authentication enforcement and session timeout policies across integrated applications

sub_controls.title: Establish an Access Granting Process
Establish an Access Revoking Process
Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
Require MFA for Remote Network Access
Require MFA for Administrative Access
Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Authentication and Authorization Systems
Centralize Access Control
Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control

controls.title: Continuous Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets within the organization's infrastructure to remediate and minimize the window of opportunity for attackers. Monitor public and private industry sources for new threats and vulnerabilities.
evidence: Authenticated vulnerability scan reports covering all in-scope systems, with scan frequency meeting policy requirements (e.g., weekly or monthly) and credentialed scan confirmation
Vulnerability remediation tracking records showing risk-rated findings, assigned owners, target remediation dates, and closure evidence aligned to SLAs
Patch management reports showing patching cadence, coverage percentage, and outstanding critical/high patches with documented risk acceptance or exception approval
Vulnerability management program documentation defining scanning frequency, remediation SLAs by severity, and exception management procedures

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
Perform Automated Application Patch Management
Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Externally-Exposed Enterprise Assets
Remediate Detected Vulnerabilities

controls.title: Audit Log Management
controls.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack. Ensure logs are stored securely and protected from modification or deletion, and that the organization has the capability to detect and investigate anomalous activity.
evidence: Log management policy defining required log sources, minimum retention period, and storage integrity controls (e.g., immutable log storage or write-once media)
SIEM platform configuration showing log source onboarding, normalization rules, and coverage of critical systems including authentication, network, endpoint, and cloud logs
Log review and alert records demonstrating analyst triage of SIEM-generated alerts and timely escalation of confirmed security events
Log integrity verification evidence, such as hash-based tamper detection reports or SIEM audit records showing no unauthorized modification of stored logs

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
Collect Audit Logs
Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
Standardize Time Synchronization
Collect Detailed Audit Logs
Collect DNS Query Audit Logs
Collect URL Request Audit Logs
Collect Command-Line Audit Logs
Centralize Audit Logs
Retain Audit Logs
Conduct Audit Log Reviews
Collect Service Provider Logs

controls.title: Email and Web Browser Protections
controls.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors by implementing controls that reduce the risk of phishing attacks, malicious web content delivery, and exploitation through the email and browsing attack surface.
evidence: Email security gateway configuration showing enabled anti-phishing, anti-spam, anti-malware, and DMARC/DKIM/SPF enforcement policies with recent threat blocking statistics
Web content filtering policy and enforcement reports showing categorized URL blocking, SSL inspection configuration, and exception management records
Phishing simulation campaign results and associated remediation training completion records for users who failed simulations
Email sandboxing or attachment detonation platform reports demonstrating analysis of suspicious attachments and URLs, with blocked threat statistics

sub_controls.title: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
Use DNS Filtering Services
Maintain and Enforce Network-Based URL Filters
Restrict Unnecessary or Unauthorized Browser and Email Client Extensions
Implement DMARC
Block Unnecessary File Types
Deploy and Maintain Email Server Anti-Malware Protections

controls.title: Malware Defenses
controls.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets through the use of endpoint protection technologies, behavioral detection capabilities, and blocking of untrusted or unauthorized content.
evidence: Endpoint protection platform deployment report showing installation coverage across all managed endpoints, servers, and workstations with agent version and signature currency
Anti-malware definition update logs confirming automated signature updates and confirmation that no endpoints have stale signatures beyond the acceptable threshold
EDR platform threat detection and response reports showing behavioral alerts, investigation actions, and containment activities for detected threats
Malware incident records including detection details, scope of impact, eradication steps, and post-incident review confirming successful remediation

sub_controls.title: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Scanning of Removable Media
Enable Anti-Exploitation Features
Centrally Manage Anti-Malware Software
Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software

controls.title: Data Recovery
controls.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state. Ensure backups are protected from destruction and tested regularly to validate data integrity and restore procedures.
evidence: Backup policy defining scope, frequency, retention period, and off-site or cloud storage requirements for all critical systems and data
Backup job completion reports demonstrating successful execution within required intervals, with failure alerting and retry evidence for missed backups
Backup restoration test records (at minimum annually) confirming successful restore of critical systems and data within defined recovery time objectives (RTOs)
Air-gapped or immutable backup storage configuration evidence demonstrating protection of backup data from ransomware or insider destruction

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Perform Automated Backups
Protect Recovery Data
Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
Test Data Recovery

controls.title: Network Infrastructure Management
controls.description: Establish, implement, and actively manage network devices to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points. Ensure network infrastructure is configured securely, segmented appropriately, and that changes follow a formal change management process.
evidence: Network architecture diagrams showing firewall placement, trust zone definitions, segmentation boundaries, and traffic flow documentation for all critical segments
Firewall rule set with documented business justification for each permitted rule, evidence of periodic review, and removal of stale or overly permissive rules
Network device hardening standards and compliance scan results demonstrating secure configuration of routers, switches, and firewalls against approved baselines
Change management records for network device configuration changes including peer review, testing, and rollback procedures

sub_controls.title: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
Securely Manage Network Infrastructure
Establish and Maintain Architecture Diagram(s)
Centralize Network Authentication, Authorization, and Auditing (AAA)
Use Secure Network Management and Communication Protocols
Ensure Remote Devices Utilize a VPN and are Connecting to an Enterprise's AAA Infrastructure
Establish and Maintain Dedicated Computing Resources for All Administrative Work

controls.title: Network Monitoring and Defense
controls.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defense against security threats across the enterprise's network infrastructure and user base, enabling rapid detection and response to network-based attacks.
evidence: IDS/IPS deployment documentation showing sensor placement at critical network chokepoints with coverage maps and signature update cadence records
Network traffic analysis reports or NDR platform alerts showing detection of anomalous traffic patterns, lateral movement, and command-and-control communications
SIEM correlation rule documentation for network-based attack patterns (e.g., port scanning, brute force, data exfiltration) with alert triage and escalation records
Network monitoring dashboard and alert response records demonstrating mean time to detect (MTTD) metrics and analyst response to network security events

sub_controls.title: Centralize Security Event Alerting
Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Detection Solution
Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
Perform Traffic Filtering Between Network Segments
Manage Access Control for Remote Assets
Collect Network Traffic Flow Logs
Deploy a Host-Based Intrusion Prevention Solution
Deploy a Network Intrusion Prevention Solution
Deploy Port-Level Access Control
Perform Application Layer Filtering
Tune Security Event Alerting Thresholds

controls.title: Security Awareness and Skills Training
controls.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness program to influence behavior among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled to reduce cybersecurity risks to the enterprise. Training must be role-appropriate and updated to address the current threat landscape.
evidence: Security awareness training program documentation including curriculum content, target audiences, delivery method, and annual update process reflecting current threats
Training completion records showing all employees completed required annual security awareness training, with remediation evidence for non-compliant users
Role-based training completion records for personnel in sensitive roles (e.g., developers, finance, IT administrators) covering topics relevant to their responsibilities
Phishing simulation campaign results showing click rates, reporting rates, and trend analysis over time demonstrating measurable improvement in workforce resilience

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
Train Workforce on Data Handling Best Practices
Train Workforce Members on Causes of Unintentional Data Exposure
Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
Train Workforce on How to Identify and Report if Their Enterprise Assets are Missing Security Updates
Train Workforce on the Dangers of Connecting to and Transmitting Enterprise Data Over Insecure Networks
Conduct Role-Specific Security Awareness and Skills Training

controls.title: Service Provider Management
controls.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data or are responsible for an enterprise's critical IT platforms or processes, to ensure these providers are protecting those platforms and data appropriately through contractual obligations and ongoing oversight.
evidence: Vendor management policy defining the third-party risk assessment process, security requirements for service providers, and periodic review cadence based on risk tier
Third-party risk assessment records for critical service providers including completed security questionnaires, SOC 2 reports, or equivalent evidence of security posture
Vendor contracts and data processing agreements (DPAs) confirming security obligations, right-to-audit clauses, breach notification requirements, and data handling standards
Annual service provider review records showing ongoing monitoring of critical vendors, including review of security incidents, changes in risk posture, and re-assessment completion

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
Establish and Maintain a Service Provider Management Policy
Classify Service Providers
Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
Assess Service Providers
Monitor Service Providers
Securely Decommission Service Providers

controls.title: Application Software Security
controls.description: Manage the security lifecycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software to prevent, detect, and remediate security weaknesses before they can impact the enterprise. Integrate security practices into the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and validate third-party components.
evidence: SAST tool integration evidence in CI/CD pipelines showing scan results, severity-based gate policies, and developer remediation records for identified vulnerabilities
WAF configuration and rule set showing protection of production web applications, with blocked attack statistics and tuning records to minimize false positives
DAST scan reports for production or pre-production web applications showing vulnerability findings, risk ratings, and remediation tracking
Software composition analysis (SCA) or code-scanning reports identifying third-party library vulnerabilities, with patching records and license compliance status
Secrets management platform implementation evidence showing elimination of hardcoded credentials in source code, with pre-commit hook or pipeline scan enforcement

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
Establish and Maintain a Process to Accept and Address Software Vulnerabilities
Perform Root Cause Analysis on Security Vulnerabilities
Establish and Maintain a Process for Receiving Security Alerts
Use Up-to-Date and Trusted Third-Party Software Components
Establish and Maintain a Severity Rating System and Process for Application Vulnerabilities
Use Standard Hardening Configuration Templates for Application Infrastructure
Separate Production and Non-Production Systems
Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
Apply Secure Design Principles in Application Architectures
Leverage Vetted Modules or Services for Application Security Components
Implement Code-Level Security Checks
Conduct Application Penetration Testing
Conduct Threat Modeling

controls.title: Incident Response Management
controls.description: Establish a program to develop and maintain an incident response capability to prepare, detect, contain, and eradicate security incidents and recover operations. The program must include defined roles and responsibilities, communication procedures, and regular testing through tabletop exercises or simulations.
evidence: Incident response plan (IRP) documentation defining incident classification taxonomy, response procedures, escalation paths, and communication templates, with evidence of annual review
Incident records for the review period showing documented containment, eradication, and recovery actions with timelines and post-incident review reports
Tabletop exercise or incident response simulation records including scenario details, participant list, identified gaps, and remediation action plan
SOAR platform playbook documentation and execution logs demonstrating automated response actions for common incident types with analyst escalation workflows

sub_controls.title: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
Assign Key Roles and Responsibilities
Define Mechanisms for Communicating During Incident Investigation
Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
Establish and Maintain Security Incident Thresholds

controls.title: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Test the effectiveness of enterprise defenses and validate that controls are operating as designed by using a comprehensive penetration testing program that includes external and internal testing, application-layer testing, and physical security testing as appropriate to the organization's risk profile.
evidence: Annual penetration test report from a qualified third-party tester covering agreed-upon scope, methodology, findings by severity, and remediation recommendations
Penetration test remediation tracking records showing assigned owners, target remediation dates, and validation retest results confirming closure of critical and high findings
Penetration testing program policy defining scope, frequency, tester qualifications, rules of engagement requirements, and escalation procedures during testing
Internal penetration test or red team exercise records including scope, methodology, findings, and corrective action plans with management sign-off

sub_controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
Remediate Penetration Test Findings
Validate Security Measures
Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
